statementLines: Passage I The best universities like Harvard and MIT, despite having the luxury of having some truly excellent teachers on their payroll, are increasingly embracing the “flipped classroom” format, where students listen to video lectures at home, and spend class time applying their knowledge, solving problems, discussing examples, etc. Professors guide that discussion and fill in wherever necessary, explaining those bits that seem to be eluding the students and throwing in advanced ideas that happen to be topical. These universities have made their video lectures available free for anyone in the world. They are also encouraging colleges and universities all over the world to integrate these online courses into their own pedagogy, picking the pieces that are appropriate for their needs and building a package around them.
Which one of the following statements best reflects the central idea of the passage given above? conventional methods. capital. and thrive well.
questions.questionText: Passage I The best universities like Harvard and MIT, despite having the luxury of having some truly excellent teachers on their payroll, are increasingly embracing the “flipped classroom” format, where students listen to video lectures at home, and spend class time applying their knowledge, solving problems, discussing examples, etc. Professors guide that discussion and fill in wherever necessary, explaining those bits that seem to be eluding the students and throwing in advanced ideas that happen to be topical. These universities have made their video lectures available free for anyone in the world. They are also encouraging colleges and universities all over the world to integrate these online courses into their own pedagogy, picking the pieces that are appropriate for their needs and building a package around them. Which one of the following statements best reflects the central idea of the passage given above? conventional methods. capital. and thrive well.

options.a: Efficacy of universities would be better in online mode of conducting classroom tuition as compared to
options.b: Availability of higher education can be made easier and cheaper without diluting the content.
options.c: We need not invest much in infrastructure related to higher education and yet develop better human and social
options.d: Private sector institutions in higher education as well as coaching institutes can take advantage of this opportunity

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **

Option b is correct: **The**passage describes how top universities like Harvard and MIT are adopting the “flipped **classroom” format**, making their video lectures available for free and encouraging other institutions to integrate these online courses. This reflects the central idea that higher education can be made more accessible and affordable without compromising on the quality of content.**

Option a is incorrect: **The passage does not claim that the efficacy of universities would be better in the online mode compared to the conventional method. Instead, it focuses on the benefits of the flipped classroom format.**

Option c is incorrect: **While the passage suggests that online resources can complement traditional education, it does not explicitly state that there is no need for investment in higher education infrastructure.**

Option d is incorrect:** The passage does not discuss the advantages for private sector institutions and coaching institutes; it focuses on the broader impact on higher education accessibility and quality.

statementLines: Passage II We live in an age where an apple can be flown thousands of miles to reach a consumer in another country. Yet food tastes best when it remains close to its roots. Long journeys and chemical preservation often dull not only the natural flavour of fresh produce but also its nutritional value. The foods people grow up with are shaped by climate, terrain, and historic trade routes, embedding cultural meaning into every bite. Even in a world where global cuisines are readily available, we continue to gravitate towards local delicacies as they speak to something deeper. They give a sense of belonging, memory, and the quiet rituals of everyday life anchored in a specific place, reminding us why local food continues to matter.
Based on the above passage, the following assumptions have been made:
I. Taste Food is deeply intertwined with a region’s culture and way of life.
II. Fresh food products retain a richer, more natural taste compared to long-stored foods.
Which of the assumptions given above is/are valid?

statementLines: Passage III Wealth creation is an art. It grows from consistent effort and strategic action, guided by clear financial goals, prudence, and attendant risk awareness. In all of this, a calm head plays a crucial role, as untamed emotions can lead to impulsive and poor financial decisions. While the mind acts as the internal architect of wealth-building, the heart helps align wealth creation with a broader purpose. The pursuit of wealth then transcends greed and self- centredness, fostering empathy, trust, and goodwill. Yet real world wealth results from tangible, measurable actions such as skills, dexterity, execution, and entrepreneurship. Trust in the hand helps build lasting fortunes and drive economic progress. When these three elements of head, heart, and hand are deployed together, the wealth so created becomes worth aspiring to, both for personal fulfillment and societal good.
Which one of the following statements best reflects the crux of the passage?

The difference between any two natural numbers A and B, both of which are less than 20, is 11. If ‘Z’ represents a set of natural numbers, which are multiples of 4 and lie between A and B, then which of the following is correct about numbers contained in set Z?

options.a: There is exactly one such number.
options.b: There are exactly two such numbers.
options.c: There can be either two or three such numbers.
options.d: There can be either one or two such numbers.

questions.explanationMarkdown: **Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer **Given that A and B are natural numbers both less than 20.**Difference between the two natural numbers A and B = 11 **We are required to count the numbers divisible by 4**lying strictly between**them. Numbers divisible by 4 and less than 20 are Are 4, 8, 12, 16, and 20**Analyze possible cases using examples **Case 1: A=1, B=12 **Numbers divisible by 4 between them: - 4-8 Total = two numbers Case 2: A=2, B=13 **Numbers divisible by 4 between them: - 4-8-12 Total = three numbers Case 3 A=3, B=14 **Numbers divisible by 4 between them: - 4-8-12 Total = three numbers Case 4: A=4, B=15 **Numbers divisible by 4 between them: - 8-12 Total = two numbers**Case 5:**A= 5, B=16 **Numbers divisible by 4 between them: - 8-12 Total = two numbers**Case 6:**A= 6, B= 17 **Numbers divisible by 4 between them: - 8-12-16 Total = three numbers**Case 7:**A= 7, B= 18 **Numbers divisible by 4 between them: - 8-12-16 Total = three numbers**Case 8:**A = 8, B= 19 **Numbers divisible by 4 between them: - 12-16 Total = two numbers**Hence, **Depending on the values of A and B, the possible** numbers contained**in set Z can**either be two or three.**Therefore, Option c is the correct answer. **

The average of four distinct non-zero positive integers w, x, y, and z is A. It is known that w exceeds the average by exactly as much as the sum of x and y falls short of twice the average. What is the value of z?
questions.questionText: Passage III Wealth creation is an art. It grows from consistent effort and strategic action, guided by clear financial goals, prudence, and attendant risk awareness. In all of this, a calm head plays a crucial role, as untamed emotions can lead to impulsive and poor financial decisions. While the mind acts as the internal architect of wealth-building, the heart helps align wealth creation with a broader purpose. The pursuit of wealth then transcends greed and self- centredness, fostering empathy, trust, and goodwill. Yet real world wealth results from tangible, measurable actions such as skills, dexterity, execution, and entrepreneurship. Trust in the hand helps build lasting fortunes and drive economic progress. When these three elements of head, heart, and hand are deployed together, the wealth so created becomes worth aspiring to, both for personal fulfillment and societal good. The average of four distinct non-zero positive integers w, x, y, and z is A. It is known that w exceeds the average by exactly as much as the sum of x and y falls short of twice the average. What is the value of z?
questions.explanationMarkdown: **Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer **Given **- Four distinct non-zero integers: w, x, y, z-Average of the four numbers = A From the definition of average: - w + x + y + z = 4A**Translating the given condition **“w exceeds the average by exactly as much as the sum of x and y falls short of twice the average” This means: - Amount by which w exceeds A = Amount by which (x + y) is less than 2A So, - w − A = 2A − (x + y) Rearranging, - w + x + y = 3A…….(i)**Find the value of z **From Equation (i): - w + x + y = 3A From the total sum: - w + x + y + z = 4A Subtracting the two equations: - z = 4A − 3A-z = A**Therefore, Option a is the correct answer. **

questions.explanationMarkdown: **Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer **Given **- Expression: 4⁴ + 4⁵ + 4⁶ + … + 4²⁰²⁶-Divisor: 6-Required: Remainder on division by 6**Observe the pattern of powers of 4 when divided by 6 **Calculate first few powers of 4: - 4¹ = 4 → remainder 4 when divided by 6-4² = 16 → remainder 4-4³ = 64 → remainder 4-4⁴ = 256 → remainder 4 So,**every power of 4 leaves the remainder 4 when divided by 6**.**Counting the number of terms **The series starts from 4⁴ and ends at 4²⁰²⁶. Number of terms = 2026 − 3 = 2023 terms**Finding the total remainder **- Each term contributes a remainder of 4-Number of terms = 2023 Total remainder contribution = 2023 × 4 = 8092**Divide the total remainder by 6 **- 8092 ÷ 6 = 1348 remainder 4 The remainder is 4**Therefore, Option c is the correct answer. **

questions.questionText: Passage III Wealth creation is an art. It grows from consistent effort and strategic action, guided by clear financial goals, prudence, and attendant risk awareness. In all of this, a calm head plays a crucial role, as untamed emotions can lead to impulsive and poor financial decisions. While the mind acts as the internal architect of wealth-building, the heart helps align wealth creation with a broader purpose. The pursuit of wealth then transcends greed and self- centredness, fostering empathy, trust, and goodwill. Yet real world wealth results from tangible, measurable actions such as skills, dexterity, execution, and entrepreneurship. Trust in the hand helps build lasting fortunes and drive economic progress. Raja has been asked to write all natural numbers from 51 to 100 (both included) and add them. He missed exactly one number by mistake and got an erroneous result. Which of the following cannot be the result he got?
questions.explanationMarkdown: **Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer **Given:**- Natural numbers from 51 to 100 are to be added-Exactly one number is missed-An incorrect sum is obtained**Find the correct sum of numbers from 51 to 100**- Number of terms = 100 − 51 + 1 = 50-Average of the numbers = (51 + 100) ÷ 2 = 75.5-Correct sum - = Number of terms × Average - = 50 × 75.5 - = 3775**Understand the effect of missing one number**- If one number is missed, the obtained sum - = 3775 − (missed number) - The smallest possible missed number = 51-The largest possible missed number = 100**Find the possible range of erroneous sums**- Maximum erroneous sum - = 3775 − 51 - = 3724-Minimum erroneous sum - = 3775 − 100 - = 3675-Therefore, the erroneous result must lie between-3675 and 3724 (both inclusive)**Check the given options**- 3699 lies between 3675 and 3724 → possible-3705 lies between 3675 and 3724 → possible-3724 lies between 3675 and 3724 → possible-3725 is greater than 3724 → not possible-The result that cannot be obtained is 3725**Therefore, Option d is the correct answer. **

40. If the sum of the digits of (p² + q + r) is itself a prime number, what is the number of possible values for the set {p, q, r}?
questions.questionText: Passage III Wealth creation is an art. It grows from consistent effort and strategic action, guided by clear financial goals, prudence, and attendant risk awareness. In all of this, a calm head plays a crucial role, as untamed emotions can lead to impulsive and poor financial decisions. While the mind acts as the internal architect of wealth-building, the heart helps align wealth creation with a broader purpose. The pursuit of wealth then transcends greed and self- centredness, fostering empathy, trust, and goodwill. Yet real world wealth results from tangible, measurable actions such as skills, dexterity, execution, and entrepreneurship. Trust in the hand helps build lasting fortunes and drive economic progress. When these three elements of head, heart, and hand are deployed together, the wealth so created becomes worth aspiring to, both for personal fulfillment and societal good. Let p, q, and r be three distinct prime numbers such that (p² + q + r) is a prime number less than 40. If the sum of the digits of (p² + q + r) is itself a prime number, what is the number of possible values for the set {p, q, r}?
questions.explanationMarkdown: **Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer **Given **- p, q, r are**three distinct prime numbers**- p² + q + r is: o a prime number o less than 40-Sum of digits of (p² + q + r) is also a**prime number**Try possible values of p **Since p² is involved and the total must be less than 40: - p = 2 → p² = 4-p = 3 → p² = 9-p = 5 → p² = 25-p ≥ 7 → p² ≥ 49 (not allowed) So only p = 2, 3, 5 are possible.**Case analysis **Case 1: p = 2 **- p² = 4-p² + q + r must be a prime < 40 Check all prime values of (p² + q + r): - For every valid prime, q + r equals an odd number-Since 2 cannot be reused and q, r must be distinct primes, -**no valid pair (q, r) exists**Conclusion:**No valid set for p = 2**Case 2: p = 3 **- p² = 9 Try prime values less than 40: - p² + q + r = 29 - → q + r = 20 - → Possible pair: 7 and 13 Check conditions: - All are distinct primes-29 is prime-Digit sum of 29 = 2 + 9 = 11 (prime) Valid set: - {3, 7, 13} Other primes: - 31 → digit sum = 4 (not prime) - 37 → digit sum = 10 (not prime)**Only one valid set found**Case 3: p = 5 **- p² = 25 Try prime values less than 40: - 29 → q + r = 4 (not possible) - 31 → q + r = 6 (not possible) - 37 → q + r = 12 (requires reuse or non-primes)**Conclusion:**No valid set for p = 5**Final count **- Total valid sets = 1**Therefore, Option a is the correct answer. **

questions.questionText: Passage III Wealth creation is an art. It grows from consistent effort and strategic action, guided by clear financial goals, prudence, and attendant risk awareness. In all of this, a calm head plays a crucial role, as untamed emotions can lead to impulsive and poor financial decisions. The pursuit of wealth then transcends greed and self- centredness, fostering empathy, trust, and goodwill. Yet real world wealth results from tangible, measurable actions such as skills, dexterity, execution, and entrepreneurship. Trust in the hand helps build lasting fortunes and drive economic progress. What is the maximum value of n such that 22 × 121 × 1331 × 2500 × 3125 × 161051 is divisible by 55n
questions.explanationMarkdown: **Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer **Given **- Expression: - 22 × 121 × 1331 × 2500 × 3125 × 161051-Divisor: - 55ⁿ**Prime factorization of 55 **- 55 = 5 × 11 So, 55ⁿ = 5ⁿ × 11ⁿ The maximum value of n depends on the**minimum power of 5 and 11**present in the given expression.**Factorize each term **- 22 = 2 × 11-121 = 11²-1331 = 11³-2500 = 2² × 5⁴-3125 = 5⁵-161051 = 11⁵**Count total powers of 11 **- From 22 → 11¹-From 121 → 11²-From 1331 → 11³-From 161051 → 11⁵ Total power of 11 = 1 + 2 + 3 + 5 = 11**Count total powers of 5 **- From 2500 → 5⁴-From 3125 → 5⁵ Total power of 5 = 4 + 5 = 9**Determine maximum value of n **- Maximum power of 11 available = 11-Maximum power of 5 available = 9 Since 55ⁿ requires both 5ⁿ and 11ⁿ, Maximum possible value of n = minimum of (11, 9) = 9**Therefore, Option d is the correct answer. **

questions.questionText: Passage III Wealth creation is an art. It grows from consistent effort and strategic action, guided by clear financial goals, prudence, and attendant risk awareness. The average age of three sisters is 17 years. If the age of the brother is included, the average age of the 4 persons remains an odd number. What can be the minimum age of the brother (in years) if his age is not less than 2 years?
questions.explanationMarkdown: **Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer **Given:**- Average age of three sisters = 17 years-Age of brother ≥ 2 years-Average age of 4 persons (3 sisters + brother) remains an odd number**Find total age of three sisters**- Average = Total age ÷ Number of persons-Therefore, Total age of three sisters = 17 × 3 = 51 years**Let the brother's age be x years**- Total age of 4 persons = 51 + x-Average age of 4 persons = (51 + x) ÷ 4**Condition for average to be an odd number**- Average of 4 persons = (51 + x) ÷ 4 = odd number Let's define an odd integer as 2k + 1. Then, (51 + x)/4 = 2k + 1-Multiply both sides by 4: 51 + x = 8k + 4-Simplify: x = 8k + 4 − 51 = 8k − 47**Find minimum x ≥ 2**- 8k − 47 ≥ 2-8k ≥ 49-k ≥ 6.125 → smallest integer k = 7-Then x = 8 × 7 − 47 = 56 − 47 = 9-Minimum age of the brother = 9 years**Therefore, Option c is the correct answer **Directions for the following 3 (three) items: **Read the following passages and answer the items that follow the passages. Your answers to these items should be **based on the passages only **

statementLines: Passage I India is at once among the fastest growing global economies and home to the largest number of malnourished children in the world. There are regions where malnutrition is not the exception but the norm. And across the country, malnutrition is the cause of death for roughly half the 1.3 million children who die before their fifth birthday each year. Even those children who survive suffer permanently from the damage that has already been done to their bodies and minds from not getting enough of the right foods and nutrients. Around 44 million children under 5 are stunted. That makes it harder for them to learn in school and subsequently earn a living as adults. Their lifetime earnings potential is almost a quarter less than that or their healthy peers.
With reference to the above passage, which of the following is/are the most rational and practical implication(s)?
I. India’s Public Distribution System should be monitored by the Union Government.
II. Girls should be encouraged to delay marriage and first pregnancy.
III. Mothers should be encouraged to breastfeed their children immediately after birth.
IV. The supply of safe drinking water and proper sanitation facilities to all should be ensured.

questions.explanationMarkdown: **Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer **Given:**- Fraction of voters who promised to vote Kushal = 3⁄7-Fraction of voters who promised to vote Vishal = 4⁄7-5% of Kushal’s promised voters switched to Vishal-P% of Vishal’s promised voters switched to Kushal-Difference in final votes = 10% of total voters**Assume a convenient total number of voters**- Since fractions are in 7, assume total voters = 70**Calculate promised votes initially**- Promised to Kushal = (3⁄7) × 70 = 30-Promised to Vishal = (4⁄7) × 70 = 40**Calculate voters who changed their decision**- From Kushal to Vishal = 5% of 30 = (5⁄100) × 30 = 1.5-From Vishal to Kushal = P% of 40 = (P⁄100) × 40 = 0.4P**Calculate final votes**- Votes received by Kushal - = 30 − 1.5 + 0.4P-Votes received by Vishal - = 40 − 0.4P + 1.5**Find the difference between votes**- Difference - = |(30 − 1.5 + 0.4P) − (40 − 0.4P + 1.5)| - = |30 − 1.5 + 0.4P − 40 + 0.4P − 1.5| - = |−13 + 0.8P| - = |13 − 0.8P|**Use the given condition**- Difference = 10% of total voters-10% of 70 = 7 So, |13 − 0.8P| = 7**Solve the equation**- Case 1: 13 − 0.8P = 7 - → 0.8P = 6 - → P = 7.5 (not an option) - Case 2: 13 − 0.8P = −7 - → 0.8P = 20 - → P = 25-The value of P = 25**Therefore, Option d is the correct answer. **

questions.explanationMarkdown: **Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer **Given:**- Speed of boat in still water = b kmph-Speed of stream (current) = c kmph-Upstream speed = b − c-Downstream speed = b + c**Use the given increase in upstream speed**- Original upstream speed = b − c-New speed of boat after increase = 1.16b-New upstream speed = 1.16b − c-Given that upstream speed increases by 24% - (b − c) × 1.24 = 1.16b − c**Form and simplify the equation**- 1.24b − 1.24c = 1.16b − c-1.24b − 1.16b = 1.24c − c-0.08b = 0.24c-Therefore, - b = 3c**Find original downstream speed**- Original downstream speed = b + c-Substituting b = 3c-Original downstream speed = 3c + c = 4c**Find new downstream speed after increase**- New speed of boat = 1.16b = 1.16 × 3c = 3.48c-New downstream speed = 3.48c + c = 4.48c**Calculate percentage change in downstream speed**- Increase in downstream speed = 4.48c − 4c = 0.48c-Percentage increase - = (0.48c ÷ 4c) × 100 - = 12% - Percentage change in downstream speed = 12%**Therefore, Option b is the correct answer. **

questions.explanationMarkdown: **Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer **Given **- Expression: 2ⁿ + 3ⁿ + 4ⁿ-Divisor: 6 -**n is a natural number **- Required: Number of**distinct remainders**Finding remainders of individual terms when divided by 6 **Consider powers separately.**When n= 1:**- 21 = 2-31 = 3-41 = 4 Remainders of (2+3+4)= 9 , when divided by 6= 3**When n= 2:**- 2² = 4-3² = 9-4² = 16 Remainders of (4+9+16)= 29 , when divided by 6= 5**When n= 3:**- 2³ = 8-3³ = 27-4³ = 64 Remainder of (8+27+64) , when divided by 6= 3**When n= 4:**- 24 = 16-34 = 81-44 = 256 Remainder of (16+81+256) , when divided by 6=5**When n= 5:**- 25 = 32-35 = 243-45 = 1024 Remainder of (32+243+1024) , when divided by 6=3**From the above trend we find that remainders for different cases of n follows a trend of:**Case 1: n is odd **Remainders of 2ⁿ + 3ⁿ + 4ⁿ when divided by 6 = 3**Case 2: n is even**remainders of 2ⁿ + 3ⁿ + 4ⁿ when divided by 6 = 5 Hence the Number of distinct remainders =**2**Therefore, Option b is the correct answer.**

questions.explanationMarkdown: **Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer **Given:**- Ratio of ages of father and mother at the time of son’s birth = 5 : 4-Six years later, the ratio of their ages becomes = 6 : 5-Son’s age at birth = 0**Assume initial ages of father and mother**- Let father’s age = 5x years-Let mother’s age = 4x years**Use the ratio given after six years**- Father’s age after 6 years = 5x + 6-Mother’s age after 6 years = 4x + 6-According to the given ratio: - (5x + 6) ÷ (4x + 6) = 6 ÷ 5**Solve the equation**- 5(5x + 6) = 6(4x + 6) - 25x + 30 = 24x + 36-x = 6**Find actual ages**- Father’s age when son was born = 5 × 6 = 30 years-Mother’s age when son was born = 4 × 6 = 24 years**Find ages after six years**- Mother’s present age = 24 + 6 = 30 years-Son’s present age = 6 years**Let after y more years, the ratio of mother’s age to son’s age become 3 : 1**- Mother’s age after y years = 30 + y-Son’s age after y years = 6 + y-According to the condition: - (30 + y) ÷ (6 + y) = 3 ÷ 1**Solve for y**- 30 + y = 3(6 + y) - 30 + y = 18 + 3y-12 = 2y-y = 6-The required number of years = 6**Therefore, Option a is the correct answer. **

**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer **Given:**- Sum of the first n terms of the AP-Sₙ = n² + 3n**Find the sum of the first (n − 1) terms**- Sₙ₋₁ = (n − 1)² + 3(n − 1) - Sₙ₋₁ = n² − 2n + 1 + 3n − 3-Sₙ₋₁ = n² + n − 2**Find the nᵗʰ**term of the AP**
- nᵗʰ term = Sₙ − Sₙ₋₁-aₙ = (n² + 3n) − (n² + n − 2)
- aₙ = 2n + 2-aₙ = 2(n + 1) **Apply the divisibility condition**

- Check values of n:
o n = 4 → 2(5) = 10 (not divisible by 7) o n = 5 → 2(6) = 12 (not divisible by 7) o n = 6 → 2(7) = 14 (divisible by 7)
- The smallest possible value of n is 6 **Therefore, Option d is the correct answer.**

questions.explanationMarkdown: **Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer **Given:**- Concentration of first wine = 10% - Concentration of second wine = 20%**Use the first mixture condition**- Ratio of mixing = m : 1-Mean concentration = 12% Using weighted average: (10m + 20 × 1) ÷ (m + 1) = 12-10m + 20 = 12m + 12-2m = 8-m = 4**Use the second mixture condition**- Ratio of mixing = 1 : n-Mean concentration = 17.5% Using weighted average: (10 × 1 + 20n) ÷ (1 + n) = 17.5-10 + 20n = 17.5 + 17.5n-2.5n = 7.5-n = 3**Find the required ratio**- m : n = 4 : 3-The value of the ratio m : n is**4 : 3**Therefore, Option a is the correct answer. **

options.a: The Question can be answered by using one of the Statements alone, but cannot be answered using the other
options.b: The Question can be answered by using either Statement alone.
options.c: The Question can be answered by using both the Statements together, but cannot be answered using either
options.d: The Question cannot be answered even by using both the Statements together.

questions.explanationMarkdown: **Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **Given **- We need to determine whether x y is an integer. - x and y are real numbers unless restricted.**Using Statement I alone **x² + y² is an integer. - Let x = √2 and y = √3-Then x² + y² = 2 + 3 = 5, which is an integer. - But x y = √6, which is not an integer. - Let x = 1 and y = 2-Then x² + y² = 1 + 4 = 5, an integer. - And x y = 2, an integer. Since x y can be integer or non-integer, Statement I alone is**not sufficient**.**Using Statement II alone **(x + y)² is an integer. - Let x = 1 and y = 0-Then (x + y)² = 1² = 1, an integer. - x y = 0, an integer. - Let x = 0.5 and y = 0.5-Then (x + y)² = 1² = 1, an integer. - x y = 0.25, not an integer. So x y can be integer or not. Statement II alone is**not sufficient**.**Using both statements together **We know the identity: (x + y)² = x² + y² + 2 x y Rearranging: 2 x y = (x + y)² − (x² + y²) From the statements: - (x + y)² is an integer-x² + y² is an integer So, 2 x y is an integer. But this does**not**guarantee that x y is an integer. - If 2 x y = 3, then x y = 1.5 → not an integer-If 2 x y = 4, then x y = 2 → integer So x y may or may not be an integer. Hence, even using both statements, the question cannot be answered.**The Question cannot be answered even by using both the Statements together.**Therefore, Option d is the correct answer. **

statementLines: Passage I Earth’s rotation feels steady, but beneath that illusion of constancy lies a subtle truth: the planet’s spin is shifting. The axis around which Earth rotates isn’t fixed. It wanders, sways, and drifts. This movement, called polar motion, happens over days, decades, and even millennia. Historically, scientists attributed these shifts to deep Earth processes like changes in the mantle or the fluid outer core. But over the past few decades, researchers have noticed that emissions due to anthropogenic activities are also playing a role. Melting glaciers, retreating polar ice, and shifting water levels are now steering the planet’s spin. Climate change has become a major driver of Earth’s rotational behavior. This quiet shift reminds us that no system on Earth is truly isolated. Climate touches everything, even the planet’s spin.

Seven friends P, Q, R, S, T, U and V are attending a musical concert and are sitting on seven consecutive chairs in a row. Only P sits between V and T; and P and Q are equidistant from R. Neither Q nor U sits at the extreme end.
Who is sitting at the centre of the row?
questions.questionText: Passage I Earth’s rotation feels steady, but beneath that illusion of constancy lies a subtle truth: the planet’s spin is shifting. The axis around which Earth rotates isn’t fixed. It wanders, sways, and drifts. This movement, called polar motion, happens over days, decades, and even millennia. Historically, scientists attributed these shifts to deep Earth processes like changes in the mantle or the fluid outer core. But over the past few decades, researchers have noticed that emissions due to anthropogenic activities are also playing a role. Melting glaciers, retreating polar ice, and shifting water levels are now steering the planet’s spin. Climate change has become a major driver of Earth’s rotational behavior. This quiet shift reminds us that no system on Earth is truly isolated. Climate touches everything, even the planet’s spin. Seven friends P, Q, R, S, T, U and V are attending a musical concert and are sitting on seven consecutive chairs in a row. Only P sits between V and T; and P and Q are equidistant from R. Neither Q nor U sits at the extreme end. Who is sitting at the centre of the row?
questions.explanationMarkdown: **Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer **Given **- 7 persons: P, Q, R, S, T, U, V-7 consecutive seats in a row-Only**P**sits between**V**and**T**-**P and Q are equidistant from R**-**Neither Q nor U**sits at any extreme end**Apply “Only P sits between V and T” **This means the arrangement must be either: - V – P – T-or-T – P – V So P is in the middle of V and T.**Apply “P and Q are equidistant from R” **If R is at some position x, then Distance(R, P) = Distance(R, Q) So P and Q must lie symmetrically on opposite sides of R. Let the distance = d Then positions must satisfy: R − d = P and R + d = Q or R − d = Q and R + d = P**Combine both conditions **Since V – P – T or T – P – V occupies**three consecutive seats**, and P must also be symmetrically placed with Q around R, R must be placed**between**P and Q. This forces R to be exactly in the middle of the 7 seats, i.e.**position 4**, otherwise symmetry cannot be maintained.**Check extreme-end condition **Q and U cannot be at the ends (positions 1 or 7). When R is at position 4, P and Q lie at equal distance on either side, which satisfies all conditions without forcing Q or U to the extremes. Valid arrangements emerge such as: - V P T R U Q S-T P V R U Q S-S Q U R V P T-S Q U R T P V In all valid cases: Position 4 = R -**R**is sitting at the centre of the row.**Therefore, Option b is the correct answer. **Directions for the following 3 (three) items: **Read the following passages and answer the items that follow the passages. Your answers to these items should be **based on the passages only **

statementLines: Passage I Cooking with biomass and coal in India is now recognized to cause major health problems, with women and children in poor populations facing the greatest risk. There are more than 10 lakh premature deaths each year from household air pollution due to polluting cooking fuels with another 1.5 lakh due to their contribution to general outdoor air pollution in the country. Although the fraction of the Indian population using clean cooking fuels, such as LPG, natural gas and electricity, is slowly rising, the number using polluting solid fuels as their primary cooking fuel has remained static for nearly 30 years at about 70 crore.
Which of the following is the most crucial and logical inference that can be made from the above passage?
questions.questionText: Passage I Cooking with biomass and coal in India is now recognized to cause major health problems, with women and children in poor populations facing the greatest risk. There are more than 10 lakh premature deaths each year from household air pollution due to polluting cooking fuels with another 1.5 lakh due to their contribution to general outdoor air pollution in the country. Although the fraction of the Indian population using clean cooking fuels, such as LPG, natural gas and electricity, is slowly rising, the number using polluting solid fuels as their primary cooking fuel has remained static for nearly 30 years at about 70 crore. Which of the following is the most crucial and logical inference that can be made from the above passage?

options.a: Rural people are giving up the use of polluting solid fuels due to their increasing awareness of health hazards.
options.b: Subsidizing the use of clean cooking fuels will solve the problem of India’s indoor air pollution.
options.c: India should increase its import of natural gas and produce more electricity.
options.d: Access to cooking gas can reduce premature deaths in poor households.

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **

Option d is correct: **Access to cooking gas can reduce premature deaths in poor households. The passage clearly indicates that the use of polluting cooking fuels causes major health problems, leading to premature deaths. Therefore, increasing access to these clean fuels can logically reduce the health risks and premature deaths associated with polluting fuels.**

Option a is incorrect: **Rural people are giving up the use of polluting solid fuels due to their increasing awareness of health hazards. The passage indicates that the number of people using polluting solid fuels has remained static for nearly 30 years. This implies that despite awareness or other factors, the use of polluting solid fuels has not significantly decreased. Therefore, this inference is not supported by the passage.**

Option b is incorrect: **Subsidizing the use of clean cooking fuels will solve the problem of India’s indoor air pollution. While subsidizing clean cooking fuels could help, the passage does not provide direct evidence to support this specific solution as the definitive answer to indoor air pollution. Therefore, this inference is not strongly supported by the passage.**

Option c is incorrect:** India should increase its import of natural gas and produce more electricity. The passage does not discuss the need for increasing imports or electricity production. It focuses on the health impacts of using polluting cooking fuels and the static number of people using them. Therefore, this inference is not directly supported by the passage.

statementLines: Passage II What happens to an electric vehicle battery when it drops to 70 per cent capacity? For an EV, that battery is no longer considered reliable. The reduced range, performance inconsistency, and safety concerns make it unsuitable for continued use in cars. But here’s the irony: at 70 per cent, that same battery still has years of usable life left. This is where the idea of second-life batteries enters the conversation. A second life battery is a type of EV battery that has been taken out of a vehicle and is being reused for stationary energy applications such as home energy storage systems, commercial backup power and solar energy storage. By redeploying these batteries, we can integrate them into more sustainable energy systems rather than allowing them to accumulate as waste. With further research and development, second-life batteries could serve as a bridge between today’s electric mobility boom and tomorrow’s circular economy.

statementLines: Passage III Each year, Olive Ridley sea turtles travel thousands of kilometers across the Indian Ocean and the Bay of Bengal, returning with astonishing precision to the very beaches where they hatched. This incredible journey is guided by one of nature’s most remarkable navigational systems, a built-in biological GPS. Researchers have discovered that sea turtles can detect a planet's magnetism and use them as navigational coordinates. Each stretch of coastline carries a unique magnetic signature, shaped by the contours of the Earth’s core and crust. When Olive Ridley hatchlings first emerge from their nests and dash to the sea, they imprint this magnetic information. Years later, as fully grown adults, they rely on this internal map to find their way back, even after spending long periods in distant feeding grounds.
Based on the above passage, the following assumptions have been made:
I. Olive Ridley sea turtles rely on Earth’s magnetic field as a primary navigational cue during long-distance migration.
II. The magnetic characteristics of coastal regions remain sufficiently stable over time for turtles to use them as reliable navigational markers.
Which of the assumptions given above is/are valid?

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer.**

Statement I is correct: **The**passage repeatedly emphasizes that turtles detect subtle variations in Earth’s **magnetism and use them as navigational coordinates.**The concept of a “biological GPS” and the reliance on magnetic signatures for returning to natal beaches directly presupposes that Earth’s magnetic field is a key navigational guide. Statement II is correct:**The**passage notes that hatchlings imprint magnetic information at birth and use it many **years later as adults.**This necessarily assumes that magnetic signatures of coastlines do not change drastically over time. questions.questionText: Passage III Each year, Olive Ridley sea turtles travel thousands of kilometers across the Indian Ocean and the Bay of Bengal, returning with astonishing precision to the very beaches where they hatched. This incredible journey is guided by one of nature’s most remarkable navigational systems, a built-in biological GPS. Researchers have discovered that sea turtles can detect a planet's magnetism and use them as navigational coordinates. Each stretch of coastline carries a unique magnetic signature, shaped by the contours of the Earth’s core and crust. When Olive Ridley hatchlings first emerge from their nests and dash to the sea, they imprint this magnetic information. Years later, as fully grown adults, they rely on this internal map to find their way back, even after spending long periods in distant feeding grounds. Ram and Shyam are standing in a long queue of more than 240 but less than 250 people. The ratio of the number of people standing ahead of Ram to the number of people standing behind Shyam is 12 : 13. Exactly how many people are there in the queue if it is known that 7 people are standing between Ram and Shyam?
questions.explanationMarkdown: **Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **Given:**- Total number of people in the queue = N, where 240 < N < 250-Number of people ahead of Ram : Number of people behind Shyam = 12 : 13-Number of people between Ram and Shyam = 7**Represent positions**- Let the number of people ahead of Ram = 12k-Then the number of people behind Shyam = 13k-The number of people between Ram and Shyam = 7**Express total number of people in the queue**- Position of Ram from front = (people ahead of Ram) + 1 = 12k + 1-Position of Shyam = (people ahead of Shyam) + 1-People behind Shyam = N − (position of Shyam) = 13k → Position of Shyam = N − 13k-Number of people between Ram and Shyam = |Position of Shyam − Position of Ram| − 1 = 7**Set up the equation**- |(N − 13k) − (12k + 1)| − 1 = 7-Simplify absolute value: |N − 25k − 1| − 1 = 7 → |N − 25k − 1| = 8-Therefore, either: 1. N − 25k − 1 = 8 → N = 25k + 9 2. N − 25k − 1 = −8 → N = 25k − 7**Check which value fits 240 < N < 250**- Case 1: N = 25k + 9 o 240 < 25k + 9 < 250 → 231 < 25k < 241 → 9.24 < k < 9.64 → k = 9 (integer) o Then N = 25 × 9 + 9 = 234 + 9 = 243 which fits the range-Case 2: N = 25k − 7 o 240 < 25k − 7 < 250 → 247 < 25k < 257 → 9.88 < k < 10.28 → k = 10 o Then N = 25 × 10 − 7 = 250 − 7 = 243 which also fits-In both cases, total number of people N = 243-Condition of 7 people between Ram and Shyam is satisfied-Total number of people in the queue = 243**Therefore, Option a is the correct answer. **

questions.explanationMarkdown: **Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **Conclusion-I: India is the world’s largest exporter of milk. - Being the largest producer of milk does not necessarily imply being the largest exporter. Production and export are different aspects; a country might produce a lot of milk but also consume most of it domestically. Therefore, this conclusion does not follow from the statement. Conclusion-II: India does not import milk. - Similarly, being the largest producer of milk does not mean that the country does not import milk. It is possible for a country to be a major producer but still import certain types of milk or dairy products to meet specific demands. Therefore, this conclusion also does not follow from the statement.
